sections.about: This metric measures the share of turnover from products/services that carry information on environmental and social parameters relevant to the product. It is a BRSR Principle 9 Essential Indicator under consumer responsibility, linked to product transparency and responsible consumption. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: JSW reported 100% across all three years, while Jindal Stainless moved from 0% in FY2022-23 to 100% in FY2023-24 and FY2024-25. Tata Steel remained partial at 50% in FY2022-23, 53% in FY2023-24 and 52% in FY2024-25. SAIL reported 0% across all three years; its BRSR discloses only a qualitative statement that it provides prescribed information for all products as required under applicable statute, without quantifying the percentage. Jindal's FY2025 position is supported by product-level disclosures, customer education, product brochures, technical visits, fabricator training, display vans, and labels carrying grade, batch, heat number, dimensions, certification logos, REACH/RoHS references and BIS/ISI marks for multiple stainless grades.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranks 3rd at 52%, behind JSW and Jindal at 100%; the gap to the best performer is 48 percentage points. Tata ranked 2nd in FY2022-23 at 50% with only JSW ahead; it slipped to 3rd in FY2023-24 despite improving to 53% because Jindal joined JSW at 100%, and remained 3rd in FY2024-25 at 52%. The underlying issue appears to be disclosure coverage and product-information mapping rather than absence of product responsibility systems, because Tata reports value-chain policy coverage, ResponsibleSteel, BIS, ISO and related certifications, but these do not translate into 100% turnover coverage for this specific metric.
sections.targets: Related BRSR commitments include Tata Steel's Net Zero by 2045 ambition, low-emission EAF transition, and scrap-use expansion, while Jindal Stainless discloses a 50% carbon-emission-intensity reduction target by 2035 versus FY2022 baseline, Net Zero by 2050, 100% supplier ESG assessment ambition, and ResponsibleSteel certification ambition.
sections.comparabilityNotes: SAIL does not provide a quantified percentage for this metric across any of the three years; its BRSR states only that it provides prescribed information as required under applicable statute, and all three values are treated as 0%. Tata Steel FY2022-23 is consolidated, while later Tata figures reflect standalone reporting.
